intro: Python's loose constraints allow quick prototyping but can lead to maintenance overhead in large applications. Moving to Kotlin introduces static safety, structural type inference, and access to the enterprise-grade Java Virtual Machine (JVM) ecosystem. Developers port codebases to Kotlin to improve compile-time correctness, streamline Android application development, or enhance concurrent throughput.

diffs.to: Static, strongly typed with advanced type inference.

diffs.from: None values can trigger runtime AttributeError / TypeError.
diffs.to: Type system explicitly separates nullable and non-nullable types.
Lightweight sequential threads via structured Coroutines.
Java Virtual Machine (JVM) bytecode, Native binary, or JavaScript.

diffs.from: Standard classes, dicts, or generic python dataclasses.

examples.title: Null Safe Operations
examples.description: Handling values that may be null without raising errors.
examples.fromCode:
def get_length(val: str | None) -> int:
    return len(val) if val is not None else 0

examples.title: Data Object Class
examples.description: Defining simple object schemas holding properties.
examples.toCode:
data class Customer(
    val id: Int,
    val email: String
)

tips.title: Enforce Null Contracts
tips.body: Differentiate between String and String? types to handle missing parameters during compilation.

tips.title: Utilize Coroutines
tips.body: Replace asyncio event loops with Kotlin Coroutines for scalable asynchronous background tasks.

tips.title: Leverage Extensions
tips.body: Extend third-party library classes using Kotlin extension functions instead of wrapping or subclassing.

tips.title: Prefer val Over var
tips.body: Declare all variable identifiers with "val" to ensure immutability unless variable reassignment is required.
